description: Filename collision: 015-graph-events-review.md (maps to DRV-015) exists in dir 03; 015-stop-on-max-iterations.md (maps to DRV-030) exists in dir 04. Both have identical basename '015-*'. Risk: filename-keyed playbook tooling collapses the two records or routes the wrong test.
recommended_fix: Rename dir 04 file to align with its DRV-ID: git mv 04--convergence-and-recovery/015-stop-on-max-iterations.md 04--convergence-and-recovery/030-stop-on-max-iterations.md

description: Filename collisions: dir 04 has 021/022/023 mapping to DRV-032/033/034; dir 05 has 021/022/023 mapping to DRV-021/022/023. Three identical-basename pairs across two dirs. Risk: filename-keyed tooling collisions; manual orchestration could load wrong test.
recommended_fix: Rename dir 04 files to align with DRV-IDs: 021->032, 022->033, 023->034. Keep dir 05 files as 021/022/023 since those match their DRV-IDs.

description: convergence.md (727 LOC) opens directly with '## 1. OVERVIEW' at line 12; no 'WHEN TO USE' section per skill_reference_template.md. Pattern repeats across references/ (see AF-0004, AF-0005).
recommended_fix: Add '## WHEN TO USE' section after H1, before OVERVIEW, with 3-5 trigger conditions (e.g. 'When evaluating whether to stop the review loop', 'When tuning convergence thresholds', 'When debugging a stuck-recovery escalation').

description: loop_protocol.md (824 LOC) opens directly with '## 1. OVERVIEW' at line 12; no 'WHEN TO USE' section per skill_reference_template.md.
recommended_fix: Add '## WHEN TO USE' section after H1, before OVERVIEW, with triggers like 'When initiating a deep-review loop', 'When resuming a paused session', 'When validating lifecycle phases'.

description: state_format.md (938 LOC) opens directly with '## 1. OVERVIEW' at line 12; no 'WHEN TO USE' section per skill_reference_template.md.
recommended_fix: Add '## WHEN TO USE' section after H1, before OVERVIEW, with triggers like 'When validating deep-review state files', 'When implementing a state consumer', 'When debugging JSONL reducer output'.

description: §1 WHEN TO USE contains 'Executor Selection Contract' subsection (lines 75-101) and 'Code-Graph Readiness TrustState Surface' subsection (lines 104-110) that document implementation detail belonging in §3 HOW IT WORKS or a reference doc, not WHEN-TO-USE activation triggers. (Note: 'FORBIDDEN INVOCATION PATTERNS' subsection at lines 55-73 IS appropriate for WHEN TO USE since it describes when NOT to use the skill — leave that in §1.)
recommended_fix: Move lines 75-101 (Executor Selection Contract) into §3 HOW IT WORKS as a new subsection. Move lines 104-110 (Code-Graph Readiness TrustState Surface) into §3 HOW IT WORKS as a new subsection. Smart Router (§2) is preserved per ADR-004 — do not edit §2.

description: Em-dashes pervasive across the skill: SKILL.md=21, references/convergence.md=6, references/loop_protocol.md=1, references/state_format.md=23, references/quick_reference.md=3, assets/deep_review_strategy.md=5, assets/deep_review_dashboard.md=5, assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl=9. Total ~73 em-dashes. HVR rules ban em-dashes (hard blocker, -5 pts each). Class-of-bug across documentation surface.
evidence: grep -c '—' across SKILL.md + references/ + assets/ = 73 instances
recommended_fix: Batch replace ' — ' with ', ' (comma + space) across all listed files. Manually review any awkward sentences post-replacement (typically tables, severity rows, definitions). Smart Router (SKILL.md §2 lines 128-310) preserved per ADR-004 — em-dashes inside §2 deferred unless edits cascade.

description: Banned word 'seamless' at line 545 ('Enable review sessions to resume seamlessly from prior state...'). HVR rules list 'seamless' as a hard-blocker word.
recommended_fix: Replace 'seamlessly' with a direct word: 'cleanly', 'reliably', or restructure sentence to drop the modifier ('Enable review sessions to resume from prior state...').

description: Orphan snippet: feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/06-resource-map-emission.md exists on disk and conforms to the snippet template, but feature_catalog.md root index has NO reference to it (root lists only 5 of 6 loop-lifecycle features at lines 51/67/83/99/115). Feature is undiscoverable from the root catalog.
recommended_fix: Add a 6th subsection in feature_catalog.md section 2 LOOP LIFECYCLE (after the Memory save entry at line ~115) for 'Resource Map Emission', with Description, Current Reality, and Source Files subsections matching the existing 5 entries' format, linking to 01--loop-lifecycle/06-resource-map-emission.md.

description: Root playbook TOC (lines 28-46) lists §1-§14 covering categories 01-06, but dirs 07 (command-flow-stress-tests, 6 files) and 08 (review-depth-v2-rollout, 6 files) exist on disk with 12 additional test files. These 12 tests have no entry in root TOC or in §14 FEATURE CATALOG CROSS-REFERENCE INDEX (line 622). Files exist but are undiscoverable from the playbook index.
recommended_fix: Add section §15 COMMAND FLOW STRESS TESTS (CP-052..057) and §16 REVIEW DEPTH V2 ROLLOUT (DRV-058..063) with per-test summaries and links. Update TOC at line 28 and §14 INDEX at line 622 to reference both new sections.

description: Two REFERENCES sections present: §5 REFERENCES (lines 450-455, 5 LOC, names router-discovered ref+asset docs) and §8 REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES (lines 498-510, 13 LOC, superset including scripts). Content overlaps; sk-doc template recommends a single REFERENCES section (or combined SMART ROUTING & REFERENCES).
recommended_fix: Consolidate: delete §5 REFERENCES (or merge its content into §8) and renumber §6-§10 down by 1, OR keep §8 as the authoritative section and demote §5 content to a one-line cross-ref pointer to §8.

description: Section headers carry packet/spec references: §9 '## 9. FINDING DEDUP (Packet 110, H-7)' (line 506) and §10 '## 10. H-9 DEFERRED' (line 534). Per `feedback_skill_docs_no_phase_references` memory, SKILL.md sections should be present-tense logic only; spec citations and 'tested in phase X' belong in changelog/v*.md.
recommended_fix: Rename §9 to '## 9. FINDING DEDUPLICATION' (drop parenthetical). For §10 'H-9 DEFERRED': either remove the section entirely (its body is a 1-paragraph planning note belonging in changelog) or rename to '## 10. EVIDENCE INTERPOLATION (future)' and rewrite the body to describe the contract without packet citations.

description: §6 SUCCESS CRITERIA (lines 456-472, 17 LOC) is underweight versus skill_md_template.md target of 80-120 LOC. Lacks completion checklist, quality-gate enumeration, validation-success criteria. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2: §6 expanded from 17 LOC to ~50 LOC with Loop Completion, Quality Gates, and Validation Success subsections.]
recommended_fix: DEFER. Adding 60+ lines of success-criteria detail would expand SKILL.md beyond its current 540 LOC and may overlap with §4 RULES or feature_catalog/. P2 deferred per ADR-002 surgical-edit policy.

description: Pre-§1 preamble lines 13-32 contains '## Convergence Threshold Semantics' subsection that documents implementation detail; per skill_md_template.md, pre-§1 content should be minimal (1-2 sentence subtitle). [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2: SKILL.md pre-§1 'Convergence Threshold Semantics' H2 subsection relocated to references/convergence.md §1 under new 'Threshold Semantics and Sibling Parity' subsection. Spec citation dropped per feedback_skill_docs_no_phase_references. SKILL.md preamble now carries a one-line cross-reference.]
recommended_fix: DEFER. Content is load-bearing for sibling packets that reference convergence threshold semantics; relocating to references/convergence.md needs cross-validation. P2 deferred per ADR-002.

description: Intro paragraph (lines 7-9) contains 3 sentences; skill_asset_template.md §3 'Title and Intro' caps at 1-2 sentences.
recommended_fix: Condense to 2 sentences: 'Auto-generated summary of the current review session, updated after each iteration. Serves as observability surface, recovery channel, and resume-decision dashboard.'

description: Template file lacks YAML frontmatter; peer markdown assets (deep_review_strategy.md, deep_review_dashboard.md) have frontmatter. Inconsistency. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2 as NOT-APPLICABLE.]
recommended_fix: DEFER. .md.tmpl files are rendered templates with token substitution; adding frontmatter may break the renderer (`prompt-pack.ts`). Verify renderer behavior before applying.
deferral_rationale: CLOSED-NOT-APPLICABLE: prompt-pack.ts (at .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts) consumes prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl via readFileSync with no frontmatter parsing. YAML workflow at deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:670 invokes it as template_path and injects the entire file contents into the rendered prompt context. Adding YAML frontmatter would inject '---' metadata into the agent's prompt. File is a renderer template, not a sk-doc asset; skill_asset_template.md conformance rules do not apply.

description: Intro paragraph (lines 5-8) contains 4 sentences; template caps at 1-2. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2 as NOT-APPLICABLE.]
recommended_fix: DEFER. Bundled with AF-0016 frontmatter deferral; intro shape will be re-evaluated when frontmatter contract is settled.
deferral_rationale: CLOSED-NOT-APPLICABLE: bundled with AF-0016 verification. Intro text is rendered into the agent's prompt verbatim; condensing it would alter prompt semantics.

description: File lacks horizontal-rule separators between H2 sections; template recommends '---' dividers. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2 as NOT-APPLICABLE.]
recommended_fix: DEFER. Bundled with AF-0016/AF-0017 deferral; render-time impact unknown.
deferral_rationale: CLOSED-NOT-APPLICABLE: bundled with AF-0016 verification. Horizontal-rule separators would inject visible '---' markers into the rendered prompt.

description: 4 early changelogs (v1.0.0.0.md, v1.1.0.0.md, v1.2.0.0.md, v1.3.0.0.md) contain a leading H1; per current sk-doc changelog convention (reference: memory `reference_sk_doc_changelog_convention_no_frontmatter`) changelogs start directly with summary paragraph (no frontmatter, no version header). The 8 newer changelogs (v1.3.1.0.md onward) follow the current convention. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2 as FALSE POSITIVE.]
recommended_fix: DEFER. Historical changelog formatting is preserved as a chronological record; rewriting historic entries risks losing version-context fidelity. P2 deferred per ADR-002.
deferral_rationale: CLOSED-FALSE-POSITIVE: re-verified against sk-doc changelog_template.md line 31 ('Files start directly with the summary paragraph - no version header or boilerplate') and template lines 47-50 which explicitly permit per-release '#### Category Name' subsections. All 12 deep-review changelogs (v1.0.0.0 through v1.8.0.0) start with summary paragraphs and use #### category subheaders correctly. Agent's claim of 'leading H1 in early changelogs' was based on counting any '^#' match in first 5 lines, conflating later '####' subsections with leading H1s.

description: Root playbook frontmatter has only 'title'; template scaffold requires 'description' field.
recommended_fix: Add 'description' field to root frontmatter (one-line summary like 'Manual testing orchestrator for deep-review: 45 tests across 8 categories with executable Setup/Steps/Expected per snippet').
deferral_rationale: CLOSED-FALSE-POSITIVE: re-verified at manual_testing_playbook.md:3 — frontmatter at line 3 carries 'description' field. Agent claim that frontmatter has only 'title' was incorrect (may have read a different file or hallucinated).

description: 6 files in dir 07 use CP-* test ID prefix (CP-052..057) instead of the playbook-wide DRV-* convention. Note: per agent verification, these files DO have §5 SOURCE METADATA (agent's claim that §5 was missing is overturned). [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2: playbook §15 now carries an explicit 'Naming Convention: CP- vs DRV-' subsection explaining that CP- denotes command-flow-pattern tests (sandboxed) and DRV- denotes review-loop behavioral tests. Convention attributed to z_archive/062-deep-loop-command-flow-stress-tests where the pattern originated.]
recommended_fix: DEFER. CP- prefix may be intentional for command-flow-stress-tests differentiation. P2 deferred pending operator confirmation of naming convention.

description: Lines 213-214 contain meta-commentary about earlier doc drafts: 'earlier drafts of this reference labelled it "Novelty Ratio", which was drift.' HVR §4 (Output Warnings) prohibits meta-commentary about the document's own revision history.
recommended_fix: Rewrite lines 213-214 as a direct statement of the current label without referencing past drafts.

description: Path-reference sweep across deep-review/ surfaces 16 .opencode/... paths that no longer resolve on disk: 4 cited by historical changelogs (v1.0.0.0..v1.3.1.0), 1 by SKILL.md (executor-config.ts under system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/), 1 by references/loop_protocol.md (executor-config.ts), 1 by references/state_format.md (coverage-graph-db.ts), and 9 by manual_testing_playbook/03..08/ tests citing system-spec-kit handlers/lib/tests that have moved. None are inside deep-review/ itself; all targets are owned by system-spec-kit or system-wide commands/. Pre-existing path rot, not introduced by this packet. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 pass 2: 12 current-doc refs migrated; 7 historical refs preserved.]
evidence: audit-findings sweep at 2026-05-23T16:30:00Z (deep-review path-reference rg sweep)
9 manual_testing_playbook tests in dirs 03/04/08 cite system-spec-kit vitest tests + handlers that moved
4 historical changelogs (v1.0.0.0..v1.3.1.0) cite deprecated speckit/ command paths
recommended_fix: DEFER. Resolution requires cross-packet investigation: locate the current home of each moved target under system-spec-kit/mcp_server/ and speckit/, then bulk-rewrite citations. Owner is the system-spec-kit packet, not this deep-review release-cleanup arc. Historical changelogs preserve original paths for chronological fidelity.
deferral_rationale: CLOSED-PARTIAL: 12 current-doc references migrated to deep-loop-runtime/ post-118 paths (SKILL.md executor-config.ts, references/loop_protocol.md + state_format.md, manual_testing_playbook tests in dirs 03/04/08), 2 speckit refs updated to .opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md, sample-040-corpus.json extension drift fixed to .jsonl. 7 historical changelog refs (v1.0.0.0..v1.3.1.0) preserved for chronological fidelity per AF-0019 historical-record policy: those refs were correct at the time the changelog was authored (pre-118 paths), and rewriting historical changelogs would lose version-context fidelity.
